issues.claim: In nineteen fifty-three, Watson and Crick figured out the structure of DNA.
issues.problem: Omits Rosalind Franklin and Maurice Wilkins. Franklin's Photo 51 X-ray diffraction data was essential to the double-helix model; modern AP framing and the College Board's emphasis on the nature of science expects acknowledgment. Wilkins shared the 1962 Nobel with Watson and Crick.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace with: 'In nineteen fifty-three, Watson and Crick — building on Rosalind Franklin's X-ray diffraction data — proposed the double-helix structure of DNA.'

issues.claim: Gene therapy has cured some forms of SCID.
issues.problem: Slightly oversimplified. Early X-linked SCID gene therapy trials (early 2000s) cured the immune deficiency but caused leukemia in several patients due to insertional mutagenesis. Newer lentiviral therapies (e.g., ADA-SCID, X-SCID with safer vectors) have produced durable cures. 'Cured some forms' is defensible but glosses over a famous AP-relevant safety/ethics case.
issues.suggested_fix: Add one beat: 'Gene therapy has cured some forms of SCID, though early trials taught hard lessons about insertional mutagenesis — newer vectors are much safer.'

issues.claim: The cost of a human genome dropped from three billion dollars in two thousand three to under a thousand dollars today.
issues.problem: The $3 billion figure refers to the total Human Genome Project cost (1990-2003), not the per-genome sequencing cost in 2003. Phrasing conflates project cost with per-genome cost. The 'under $1,000' modern figure is correct (sub-$200 now via Illumina/Ultima).
issues.suggested_fix: Clarify: 'The Human Genome Project cost about three billion dollars to finish the first human genome in two thousand three; today an individual genome can be sequenced for under a thousand dollars.'

overall_assessment: Module is factually solid and AP-aligned — PCR temperatures, Taq/Thermus aquaticus, EcoRI palindrome and sticky ends, gel electrophoresis polarity, Sanger ddNTP/3'-OH mechanism, CRISPR guide RNA + Cas9 + NHEJ/HDR repair, and Nobel attributions are all correct. Only minor polish items: credit Rosalind Franklin in the hook, soften the SCID 'cured' claim, and distinguish HGP total cost from per-genome cost. Safe to ship as-is; the suggested fixes would lift it from solid to exemplary AP rigor.
